title: HTTP Semantics, Roles, Representations, and Layered Evidence · Browser and Node Client Systems · Contract Lens: Stateless Application State
summary: An accessible operations dashboard calls a browser API through service workers and gateways, but one visible error collapses CORS, network, status, and schema failures. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts stateless application state increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch protocol evidence map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain protocol statelessness while locating cookies, tokens, caches, and workflow state outside core request semantics. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Trace request construction, routing, transport, intermediary handling, response interpretation, and cleanup as distinct stages. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
